Lecture2/02_00_Introduction.reason: Motivational paragraph introducing Lecture 2 topics (localization, Dedekind domains). Claims: none. Pure narrative overview with no precise mathematical statements.

Lecture2/02_00a_Discussion.notes: Section 2.1 body. Claims: (1) universal property of localization, (2) equivalence relation construction of S⁻¹A, (3) ι(S) ⊆ (S⁻¹A)×, (4) uniqueness of factoring map, (5) for integral domains: (a,s)~(b,t) iff at-bs=0, (6) S⁻¹A embeds in Frac(A), (7) S⊆T implies S⁻¹A ⊆ T⁻¹A, (8) ι injective when A integral domain, (9) A ⊆ S⁻¹A ⊆ Frac(A). Most are in Mathlib but formalizable.

Lecture2/02_00b_Discussion.notes: Section 2.2 body. Claims: (1) contraction φ⁻¹(b) is an A-ideal, (2) extension aB = (ab : a∈a, b∈B) for injective φ, (3) a ⊆ a^ec, (4) b^ce ⊆ b, (5) if a∩S ≠ ∅ then a^e = B, (6) when B = S⁻¹A: b^ce = b.

Lecture2/02_01_Theorem.notes: Stage 3.2 review passed. All 4 claims mapped: (1) bijection via orderIsoOfPrime, (2) forward map via comap_map_of_isPrime_disjoint, (3) inverse map via isPrime_of_isPrime_disjoint, (4) round-trip via map_comap. No definition-level sorries. No gaps.

Lecture2/02_02_Remark.notes: Remark 2.2 scaffolded. 4 claims: (1) generators of a generate a^e — Ideal.map_span recall, (2) every B-ideal is a^e — IsLocalization.map_comap recall, (3) noetherian preserved — IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing recall, (4) PID preserved — custom theorem with sorry.

Lecture2/02_02a_Discussion.notes: Localization at prime. Claims: (1) A_p = S⁻¹A for S = A\p, (2) prime ideals of A_p biject with primes of A contained in p, (3) pA_p is unique maximal ideal, (4) A_p is a local ring.

Lecture2/02_03_Remark.notes: Warning 2.3 scaffolded. Counterexample: 9/3 = 3/1 in Z_(3) even though 3 in (3). All proofs completed (no sorry).

Lecture2/02_05a_Discussion.notes: Section 2.3 body. Claims: (1) universal property of S⁻¹M, (2) S⁻¹M ≅ M ⊗_A S⁻¹A, (3) ι injective iff ×s injective for all s∈S, (4) for A subring of field K with M ⊆ K-vector space, M embeds in S⁻¹M.

Lecture2/02_12a_Discussion.notes: Main claim: integral closure of Dedekind domain in finite separable extension is Dedekind. Fully covered by Mathlib (IsIntegralClosure.isDedekindDomain). Examples: ℤ, k[X].

Lecture2/02_20a_Discussion.notes: Claims: (1) in a Dedekind domain every nonzero fractional ideal is invertible (forward ref to next lecture), (2) this is not true in general.

Lecture2/02_21_Example.notes: Subring A = Z+2iZ defined with carrier {z : GaussianInt | Even z.im}. All 5 claims addressed. 3 sorries remain in proofs.

Lecture2/02_21a_Discussion.notes: Section 2.6 intro. Claims: (1) I invertible iff I(A÷I)=A, (2) nonzero fractional ideals form abelian monoid, (3) invertible fractional ideals form abelian group.
